key_insight: The paper proposes a sequential replica exchange with solute tempering (REST) workflow that grows a supramolecular polymer one monomer at a time to efficiently predict atomistic binding structures.
engineering_relevance: This approach reduces the replica-sampling burden of global REST and is better suited for scalable, detailed modeling of self-assembling polymer structures in explicit solvent.
method: Add monomers sequentially to the growing polymer model.
Apply REST only to the newly added monomers to search for favorable binding positions.
Use an energy-based scoring function and repeat until the target chain length is reached.
evidence_signal: In an explicit-solvent model supramolecular polymer test, the method reproduced characteristic hydrogen-bonding patterns while improving replica traversal and lowering computational cost.
watch_item: The method assumes one-dimensional polymerization, so performance may be limited for systems with branching, competing morphologies, or non-sequential assembly pathways.
paper_title: Sequential replica exchange with solute tempering for atomistic modeling of supramolecular polymer structures
